story_name: Bell watcher subscribes to gift wraps
implementation_notes: subscribeDirectMessageNotifications now opens two NDK subscriptions: kind-4 (legacy NIP-04 DMs) and kind-1059 (NIP-17 gift wraps). Two separate subscriptions with distinct filter objects per pre-06 decision.
The kind-1059 handler is async and wrapped in try/catch, so unwrapAndOpen failures are silently swallowed without leaking unhandled promise rejections (AC-17).
seenRumorIds: Set<string> keyed by inner rumor.id (NOT outer event.id) prevents double-bump on relay redelivery (AC-19). The kind-4 path continues to key on event.id unchanged.
DirectMessageNotificationsWatcher.tsx passes privateKeyHex (from useNostrIdentity()) as the third parameter — no new state or context needed.
The kind-1059 handler silently skips non-kind-14 rumors (kind-7, kind-444, kind-21059) with no logger call, per spec §3.2 and D3 decision.
Info-level logger calls added at dm:rumor-rejected (shouldIngestRumor false) and dm:unwrap-failed (unwrap throws). No console.warn/console.error anywhere in the new path (AC-31).

Unit tests use a FakeSub with an explicit .on() method mirroring NDKSubscription.on — the sub must return an object that has .on() as a method, not just optional property access (sub.on?.('event', handler) — fixing the root cause of the initial test failures).
emitEvent() is async-aware and awaits all handler results before returning, so post-handler assertions (rememberContact, incrementDirectMessage calls) are settled before the test asserts.

files_changed.change: ADDED second NDK subscription (kind-1059) and kind1059Handler; ADDED seenRumorIds Set; ADDED privateKeyHex parameter; ADDED logger (createLogger from logger.ts)
NEW — 9 tests covering AC-15, AC-16, AC-17, AC-18 (3 variants), AC-19, AC-30, AC-31
NEW — two-context E2E test for AC-22 (alice closed-chat bell, bob sends gift-wrapped DM)
NEW — test count baseline before changes
NEW — module contract documenting exported symbols, new subscriptions, handler contract

ac_coverage.AC-14: TypeScript compile check — privateKeyHex accepted in subscribeDirectMessageNotifications params

ac_coverage.AC-16: ✓ Unit test: kind-1059 event with kind===14, pubkey!==own, created_at>lastRead → rememberContact + incrementDirectMessage called once
ac_coverage.AC-17: ✓ Unit test: unwrap throws → no side effects, no unhandled rejection
ac_coverage.AC-18: ✓ Unit test (3 variants): kind-7, kind-444, kind-21059 rumors → no side effects
ac_coverage.AC-19: ✓ Unit test: same rumor id, different outer id → incrementDirectMessage called exactly once
ac_coverage.AC-20: ✓ Code inspection: DirectMessageNotificationsWatcher passes privateKeyHex from useNostrIdentity()
ac_coverage.AC-21: ✓ Unit test file exists and covers AC-15 through AC-19
ac_coverage.AC-22: ✓ E2E spec written: dm-giftwrap-bell.spec.ts (two-context scenario, alice closed-chat, bob sends gift wrap)

ac_coverage.AC-31: ✓ Unit test: no console.warn or console.error from any silent-skip path

spec_questions_answered.pre-01: YES — separate seenRumorIds Set<string> keyed by rumor.id alongside existing seenMessageIds Set<string> keyed by event.id. Safe per D7 argument (disjoint id namespaces with no collision risk), explicit per spec 'add a parallel seenRumorIds'.
spec_questions_answered.pre-02: YES — async handler inside try/catch catches all exceptions including unwrap failures.
spec_questions_answered.pre-03: YES — two separate ndk.subscribe calls with distinct filter objects.
spec_questions_answered.pre-04: YES — seenRumorIds is a new Set<string> in module-scope closure alongside existing seenMessageIds.
spec_questions_answered.pre-05: YES — two separate NDK subscriptions with distinct filter objects (kind-4 and kind-1059).
spec_questions_answered.pre-06: YES — single cleanup function calls .stop() on both kind4Sub and kind1059Sub.
spec_questions_answered.pre-07: YES — privateKeyHex from useNostrIdentity() passed as third parameter.
spec_questions_answered.pre-08: YES — new test file created from scratch. Fake NDK with emitEvent helper; vi.mock patterns from codebase (unwrapAndOpen, shouldIngestRumor, incrementDirectMessage, rememberContact).

NDK subscription stub required an explicit .on() method (not just optional property access) on the fake sub object — initial test failures traced to the test mock's sub object not having .on as a callable, only as an optional property, causing the handler never to be registered.
